VC/PE (e.g., General Atlantic $108B, Providence, Accel) lower due to smaller scale vs public AM giants.
Persons/families/endowments excluded from ranking as non-companies.
Slight AUM variances across sources but relative order consistent; 2026 data aligns with prior trends.

ranking.justification: BlackRock ranks first as the world's largest asset manager with approximately $14 trillion AUM<grok:render type="render_inline_citation"><argument name="citation_id">0</argument></grok:render><grok:render type="render_inline_citation"><argument name="citation_id">20</argument></grok:render>, global operations, institutional reach via Aladdin platform (systemic infrastructure), and fiduciary to vast capital flows, outranking all others in scale and centrality.

Vanguard ranks second with ~$12 trillion AUM<grok:render type="render_inline_citation"><argument name="citation_id">0</argument></grok:render><grok:render type="render_inline_citation"><argument name="citation_id">20</argument></grok:render>, dominant passive indexing for 30M+ investors, global footprint, but below BlackRock due to slightly smaller AUM and less diversified active/infrastructure tooling.

FMR LLC (Fidelity) third with ~$7T AUM<grok:render type="render_inline_citation"><argument name="citation_id">20</argument></grok:render>, brokerage/asset mgmt for institutions/retail, but below Vanguard due to smaller scale; ranks above State Street as primary allocation/management exceeds pure custody emphasis.

State Street fourth with ~$4T AUM + ~$40T+ custody/AUC (core plumbing)<grok:render type="render_inline_citation"><argument name="citation_id">40</argument></grok:render>, institutional focus, global servicing, but below FMR due to lower AUM despite superior infrastructure; above Geode via broader custody/ETF scale.

Geode fifth with ~$1.9T AUM systematic beta (index-like), Fidelity affiliate, broad equity/niche reach, but below State Street as specialized passive lacks custody/infra breadth and smaller relative to top tiers.

recommended_focus_set.reasoning: These top 5 exhibit the highest leverage over capital flows via massive AUM/custodial scale, global/institutional centrality, and infrastructure roles, warranting deepest ownership/investor tracing despite Capital Infra classification (override for systemic priority).
